item.title: Portland sieges ICE facility
item.description:
Portland isn't getting coverage because their Resistance is stronger. The propaganda media doesn't want people to understand that there are more of us than there are fascists. They don't want you to understand how easy it could be. The Revolution will not be televised.
Days prior to the siege.

Like what we do and how we do it? Leave a tip. Or don't. It's totally cool either way. Stay True & Stay Tuned - Meta Pathos

item.title: Heck yeah!! Feeling Good
item.description: It was out of the blue, and I heard her talking to her friends. I was walking around the corner and overhear them. I turned around and did a little dance. Booya. Obligatory dad joke - What are Chickens favorite shoe brand....? Rebawks F.p. edit. Ohh wow!! And I get a bunch of likes on imgur!! This is too much. Keep it up to all the parents out there. Everything is completely bonkers these days and we're on here memeing away. Stay weird my friends.

item.title: John Candy being a bro.
item.description: While filming Planes, Trains and Automobiles in 1987, Steve Martin recalled how John Candy’s quiet kindness and patience deeply impacted the atmosphere on set. The shoot, set during a brutal Midwest winter, involved icy winds, deep snow, and long days in rural Illinois—conditions that wore down even the toughest professionals. While most actors took refuge in heated trailers between takes, Candy often remained outside, chatting with crew members, offering encouragement, and handing out fresh coffee he’d arranged himself after realizing the only option was the lukewarm brew from the catering truck. Candy’s compassion wasn’t a one-time gesture. During Uncle Buck in 1989, he noticed that many younger crew members had no choice but to eat snacks from vending machines because the budget focused on providing meals for lead talent. Without making a scene or notifying producers, Candy hired a hot food truck at his own expense, ensuring the entire team had something warm and substantial to eat. He believed no one should work hard under tough conditions and go unfed. Chris Columbus, who directed Candy in Only the Lonely in 1991, shared how Candy learned every crew member’s name by the third day of shooting. Each morning began with personal greetings, and each night ended with a handshake and a thank you. When a crew member’s birthday was overlooked by production, Candy quietly organized a surprise cake and music between scenes to make sure the day didn’t go uncelebrated. His approach came from humble beginnings in Toronto’s Second City during the 1970s, where Candy juggled multiple roles—from hauling equipment to cleaning stages—long before fame found him. That experience instilled a deep respect for every job on a set, no matter how seemingly small. While filming Splash in 1984, a sudden downpour at a beach location threatened to destroy cameras and other gear. As the cast and directors rushed to shelter, Candy stayed behind to help the tech crew save their equipment. Daryl Hannah later shared how his jokes and presence helped ease tension—and how his hands-on help saved thousands of dollars in gear. In The Great Outdoors (1988), a nighttime shoot stretched into the early morning. As Candy noticed freezing crew members shivering in light jackets, he quietly ordered portable heaters and blankets from a local supplier, covering the cost himself. To him, no one should have to suffer when a solution was within reach. He also refused preferential treatment when others were left out. On a flight for a remote shoot, the stars were seated in first class while the crew was crammed into coach. When Candy learned this, he gave up his seat to a weary camera assistant and sat in the back of the plane without mentioning it to anyone. Those who worked with Candy consistently spoke of how he never let celebrity go to his head. He’d give up his trailer if wardrobe needed space or help fund overtime for exhausted crews. His actions weren’t for headlines—they were quiet affirmations of his values. To Candy, true success meant uplifting the people behind the scenes. He never sought recognition for these acts, never mentioned them in interviews or speeches. He simply did what he felt was right. For those who knew him, Candy’s legacy isn’t just built on his talent—it rests on the warmth, generosity, and humanity he showed to the people the industry often overlooks. In a business obsessed with stars, John Candy made it his mission to honor the people who helped make the magic happen.
item.dc:creator: Lassannn

item.title: The advisors were friendly and talkative.
item.dc:creator: Iullyiullyus

item.title: What Happened Today #141
item.description: Trump versus the US is institutional trench warfare, and his forces captured some ground today. CFPB abandoned 18 enforcement cases protecting consumers from predatory lenders while Voice of America was gutted again with 85% workforce cuts. Social Security is crumbling and has started hiding its telephone wait times to conceal its collapse. The judiciary keeps fighting back and blocked his Harvard foreign student ban and some NSF funding cuts. This war is already happening. They seize agencies, courts push back, and they advance elsewhere while judges and civil servants defend what ground they can. Tell your representative and senators to get in the fray so that we can reinforce the institutions that are still standing. Don't rely on the algorithm to keep you informed. Let me send this to you every night, free and private, so that you always know what's going on. Add your email at https://govbrief.today or find us on Substack at Gov Brief Today. #GovBriefToday #Resist 1. https://go.govbrief.today/trump-us-nato-5percent 2. https://go.govbrief.today/judge-posse-comitatus 3. https://go.govbrief.today/vance-padilla-democrats 4. https://go.govbrief.today/texas-student-protests 5. https://go.govbrief.today/scotus-tarriff-accelerate 6. https://go.govbrief.today/cfpb-drops-cases 7. https://go.govbrief.today/khalil-ordered-released 8. https://go.govbrief.today/jackson-dissent-moneyed-interests 9. https://go.govbrief.today/parliamentarian-stops-bill-provisions 10. https://go.govbrief.today/scott-confirmed-cbp 11. https://go.govbrief.today/guevara-journalist-removal 12. https://go.govbrief.today/commandments-law-blocked 13. https://go.govbrief.today/harvard-foreign-students-until-decision 14. https://go.govbrief.today/judge-blocks-nsf-funding-cut 15. https://go.govbrief.today/usps-doge-whitehouse 16. https://go.govbrief.today/union-wildfire-fighters 17. https://go.govbrief.today/ssa-call-wait-times 18. https://go.govbrief.today/mass-layoffs-agm 19. https://go.govbrief.today/rose-garden-bulldozed 20. https://go.govbrief.today/trump-peace-prize Yesterday's Top Story - https://go.govbrief.today/truett-advocate-violence Fighting Back - https://go.govbrief.today/dodgers-pledge-one-million Get Free, Private Email With This News Daily - https://govbrief.today
item.dc:creator: GeorgeBounacos

item.title: Well, this is certainly new to me
item.description: https://www.msn.com/en-us/news/world/first-methane-powered-sea-spiders-found-crawling-on-the-ocean-floor/ar-AA1GU5PR I've heard of all sorts of spiders, including the barking kind, but this is new to me.
